descrição: The fund specifically allocated to cover losses caused by platform issues, ensuring swift and full user protection.
A core MEXC value proposition referring to zero trading fees on eligible spot and futures trades, extended to specific trading pairs or event periods during campaigns.
A recurring MEXC promotion offering 0% Maker and Taker fees on eligible Spot and Futures trading pairs.
Under MEXC-AI. Analyzes user's current asset portfolio and gives users some insights and suggestions.
AI-generated analysis section within the MEXC-AI interface.
A copy trading feature powered by AI models that automatically generates and executes trading strategies based on algorithmic analysis.
An AI-powered tool that continuously scans and analyzes the latest cryptocurrency market news from social media feeds and developments, providing real-time insights and updates relevant to traders.
The entrance to the AI Recommendation List.
A curated list of selected trading pairs recommended by the AI based on market trends, volatility, and trading potential, designed to help users identify promising investment opportunities.

An AI-driven trade technical indicator signal alert system that pushes timely notifications about potential buy/sell opportunities, market anomalies, or risk warnings to help users make informed trading decisions. This is a feature under AI Analysis.
AI Toolbox is a centralized hub where users can access all AI-powered features and tools across MEXC in one place.
Platforms or services integrated with MEXC's API for copy trading, bots, or asset management, earning commissions on user trades.
An API key is a unique code that allows external applications to securely access your account and interact with MEXC services according to the permissions you grant.
Institutional users on MEXC using APIs for high-frequency trading, benefiting from interest-free loans, higher rate limits, and liquidity incentives.
A reward voucher that increases the annual interest rate on eligible Flexible Savings or Futures Earn products. Sometimes referred to as Earn APR booster.
An error message displayed when a user attempts to access a feature or page they are not eligible for, such as due to regional restrictions or incomplete verification.
Under this section, select asset/margin modes available for managing account assets.
Status indicating a prediction was correct.
A button or action that enables a feature, account status, or service.
The trigger price at which a conditional order becomes active and starts executing.
A confirmation message displayed when a feature, service, or account has been successfully activated.
The timestamp when the grid bot was activated.
Generally displayed as a section label in the orders tab. An order that is currently open and not yet completed or cancelled.
The action of depositing additional funds into a position to increase its margin and reduce liquidation risk.
Extra security measures, such as additional codes or documents, required for sensitive operations to confirm user identity and prevent fraud.
Higher-tier identity verification requiring additional documents for full access to platform features and higher limits.
A leverage mode that allows different leverage and margin settings to be configured independently for long and short positions.
A Futures feature that lets traders set customized take-profit and stop-loss levels for more precise risk management.
Often shortened to "ad." A listing created by a P2P seller to buy or sell cryptocurrency.
A user who posts a buy/sell offer on the P2P platform.
A label to describe the Trader's style: Trading approach involves higher risk and larger position sizes in pursuit of greater potential returns.
A reward distributed as free tokens directly to a user's account.
A MEXC campaign where users earn crypto rewards by completing simple deposit and trading tasks across hundreds of projects.
The Alpha trading zone platform for trading promising on-chain projects before mainstream markets.
Alpha Strategy mode: responses provide more professional analysis. It is a feature under MEXC-AI.
Any cryptocurrency other than Bitcoin.

contexto: MEXC AI. [Product: AI Analysis | UI: Analysis]
descrição: A comprehensive technical analysis module powered by AI that interprets various technical indicators and chart patterns to generate actionable market insights for traders. Official product name is "AI Analysis," but displayed as "Analysis" in the UI.
A user-set code included in legitimate platform emails to help identify and avoid phishing attempts.
A formal request submitted to MEXC Customer Service to resolve a trade issue, where agents review evidence and determine an outcome.
The party who initiates an appeal to request platform intervention in a disputed transaction.
The party responding to an appeal in a disputed transaction.
Refers to arithmetic progression or evenly spaced intervals.
The lowest sell price currently available in the order book, representing the best ask from sellers.
A mobile application that generates time-based one-time passwords (TOTPs) for two-factor authentication (2FA).
A feature that automatically transfers available margin to an isolated position when it approaches the liquidation threshold.
Feature that automatically stakes idle assets into Flexible Savings to earn returns.
When abbreviated, always written as ADL (all caps). A mechanism where, after liquidation, remaining positions are taken over by the exchange's system.
A decentralized trading protocol that uses algorithms and liquidity pools to determine asset prices instead of traditional order books.
System-managed setting automatically adjusting parameters such as slippage.
Amount of tokens available for trading or withdrawal.
The amount of funds remaining in an account that can be used to open new positions or support existing ones.
The weighted average price of multiple closing trades for a position.
The weighted average price of multiple entries into the same position direction.
Generally displayed as a data field in holdings. Average price at which tokens were bought in held positions.
An institutional tier in the MEXC P2P Merchant Program for business-focused entities.
The amount of tokens or assets currently held in an account or wallet.
The price at which a position's equity becomes zero after exhausting all margin, triggering potential auto-deleveraging if the position cannot be liquidated profitably.
The base interest rate of a MEXC Earn product when it consists of a base interest rate and a bonus APR.
The cryptocurrency being bought or sold in a trading pair, with its price quoted in another currency.
The highest buy price currently available in the order book, representing the best bid from buyers.
Contract feature restricting certain addresses from transactions.
A non-withdrawable type of reward, specifically a Futures bonus used only for opening positions.
The bonus APR of a MEXC Earn product when it consists of a base interest rate and a bonus APR.
A sub-account managed or linked to a broker for facilitating trades, often used in copy trading or managed investment services on crypto platforms.
Copy Trade Showdown's level-2 treasure chest reward.
Generally displayed as a data field in order details. Price at which tokens were bought.
A contract or position that gives the holder the right (but not the obligation) to buy an asset at a specified price within a certain time period.
A chart composed of multiple candlesticks arranged over time to display price trends and market behavior.
Indicates that payment is made by depositing physical cash directly into the seller's bank account at a bank branch or deposit machine.
A dedicated section within MEXC P2P for cash-in-person trades, accessible under the VND market.
Indicates that payment is made face-to-face using physical cash rather than through electronic transfer.
An order type that automatically adjusts the limit price to chase the market price within a specified range, improving order fill probability.
A daily action where users complete a required activity to claim rewards, with consecutive check-ins forming a streak that unlocks greater rewards.
A reward box earned based on trading achievements or competition performance. Commonly seen in M-Day and Copy Trade Showdown events.
The amount of a token currently available and circulating in the market.
Initiates the action of collecting an available reward or incentive.
The action of exiting an open position by placing an opposing trade, realizing PNL.
Generally displayed as an action button. Action to simultaneously close all open positions at the current market price.
An action to sell and close an existing long position. Sometimes shown as "Close Long (Sell)".
The process of offsetting an open position to exit the trade and realize PNL.
The price at which a position is closed, determining the final realized PNL.
An action to buy and close an existing short position. Sometimes shown as "Close Short (Buy)".
The realized profit or loss calculated upon closing a position, including trading fees, funding fees, and the difference between entry and exit prices.
Coin-Margined. Inverse perpetual futures margined in the base cryptocurrency, with PNL in the coin.
An asset pledged to secure a loan or leveraged position, which may be liquidated if obligations are not met.
A percentage of a referred user's trading fees earned by the referrer. Primarily used in the MEXC Referral Program and Affiliate Program.
The amount of funds pledged toward a Launchpad subscription during the commitment period.
Physical assets, such as gold, silver, or oil, that can be traded on crypto platforms using digital assets as collateral.
Feature name. Select and compare multiple crypto tokens side by side.
The standard unit of a futures contract on MEXC, representing a fixed value (e.g., 1 cont = 0.0001 BTC for BTC futures), used to measure position size.
Generally displayed as a data field in Event Futures. The total monetary value of Event Futures contracts traded or held.
For perpetual futures, no expiry; instead, funding fees are settled periodically to anchor prices.
The nominal value of one contract unit, e.g., 1 cont = fixed USD or coin amount, defining position scale.
Verification status confirming the token’s smart contract source code is valid.
The total number of Event Futures contracts held or traded.
Generally displayed as a data field in Convert. The current exchange rate between the from-coin and to-coin used for the conversion calculation.
A feature that allows users to quickly convert one cryptocurrency to another instantly without order book matching.
A trading feature allowing Followers to automatically copy the trades of a Trader in real time.
A competition event where Traders and Followers compete based on trade volume and performance.
A special type of trial fund designated exclusively for copy trading. Once claimed, it can be used to participate in Futures copy trading.
Button for generating a new trading bot with user defined parameters, such as investment amount, grid size, and price range.
Margin mode where all positions share margin balance collectively.
Technology that enables interaction, asset transfer, or communication between different blockchain networks.
Cross-region arbitrage is the practice of profiting from price differences for the same asset across markets in different geographic regions.
The type of cryptocurrency asset involved in the conversion.
Generally displayed as a data field on token pages. The latest market capitalization value of a token.
Generally displayed as a data field on token pages. The last traded price or prevailing market price of a token.
Generally displayed as a section label in the trading interface. Open token positions currently held by the trader.
Generally displayed as a data field in the comparison view. The tokens currently selected and included in the comparison.
Generally displayed as a parameter setting label. User-defined setting allowing customization of trading parameters.
Generally displayed as a parameter setting label. User-defined percentage quantity to buy in an order.
Generally displayed as a parameter setting label. User-defined percentage quantity to sell in an order.
Support services available to assist users with platform and account-related questions.
A Spot DCA plan automatically buys a set amount of cryptocurrency at regular intervals within a user-defined price range and schedule.
Official name of the MEXC decentralized exchange or DEX+ platform.
Generally displayed as a data field in the trading interface. The profit or loss achieved by the user in a single day, measured in USDT.
The daily cost of borrowing for Fixed Loans, applied to the outstanding loan amount. Any period less than one full day is counted as a full day.
Top Traders selected for the Followers.
An application that runs on a blockchain or decentralized network rather than centralized servers.
Trading platforms that allow users to exchange cryptocurrencies directly from their wallets without intermediaries.
The process of removing a cryptocurrency or token from an exchange's trading pairs.
Simulated trading environment using virtual funds to practice strategies without real finullcial risk, available on MEXC for futures and spot.
Initiates the action of depositing assets into the user's account.
Copy Trade Showdown's level-5 treasure chest reward.
India's central bank digital currency (CBDC), a blockchain-based digital version of the Indian Rupee for secure, efficient transactions.
A digital wallet specifically for holding, transacting, and managing India's central bank digital currency (CBDC), the eRupee, enabling seamless digital payments.
A user or affiliate directly invited by an existing member, representing the first level in a referral or affiliate hierarchy, from which commissions are earned.
The trading bias or strategy direction (e.g., long or short), indicating the expected market move.
A disagreement between buyer and seller requiring intervention.
An investment strategy where a fixed amount is invested at regular intervals, reducing the impact of price volatility over time.
A prediction that the underlying asset's price will decrease during the contract period.
The rate of return a trader receives if the asset price goes down and their prediction in Event Futures is correct.

contexto: Specific benefit/incentive provided by the Alpha program
descrição: Access to trade tokens in Alpha before they become widely available.
Platform’s mechanism to close or adjust positions ahead of corporate events like splits or dividends.
Navigates the user to an Earn product.
The current account assets available as margin for opening Futures positions, valued in USD.
A higher-tier prize pool in Flip Fest for participants who meet higher criteria than the Rising Pool.
Confirmation of email address ownership by sending and entering a code or clicking a link.
The action of initiating interest accrual on a Hold and Earn product.
Refers to the full size of an open position, as opposed to a partial portion.
The price at which a position was opened, used for PNL calculations.
The total value of assets in the account, including unrealized profit and loss.
Displayed on Wallets page in App. Total valuation of assets held within the DEX+ account.
A security mechanism that holds the seller's crypto in trust until the buyer's payment is confirmed, protecting both parties.
Short for 'Estimated.' The period at the end (Est.) is part of the standard abbreviation and should always be included.
The projected annual return on a MEXC Earn product.
The projected profit or loss on an open position, calculated in real time based on current market prices.

termo: Estimated profit per grid (Est. profit per grid)
descrição: The projected profit or loss calculated based on current market conditions and position parameters.
The projected profit expected from each grid interval in a grid trading strategy or bot operation.

Derivative contracts that allow users to speculate on the outcome of specific real-world events (e.g., economic reports or sports) using a simplified "Yes/No" mechanic, where settlement is based on whether the predicted event occurs.
The rate at which one currency is exchanged for another in interest distribution or repayment.
Expected PNL involves expectations, should be used in prediction related trading that needs to factor in risk adjustment.
Frequently Asked Questions. A section providing answers to common user queries. Always use the abbreviation FAQ — do not spell out the full form or use alternative shortenings.
Advanced biometric authentication using facial recognition technology for quick and secure access to accounts and features.
A biometric authentication method that verifies user identity by scanning facial features.
Error message shown when content cannot be retrieved. (Standard term; do not use 'Loading failed'.).
The calculated real-time price based on index and market data, used for PNL and liquidations to prevent manipulation. Used in place of mark price.
A user-curated list of preferred or watched tokens.
Highlights of selected content / trading pairs presented to users in the interface.
A prioritized listing that appears at the top of search results for greater visibility. Featured ads may require a fee, special merchant status, or be granted through promotions.
A voucher that can be applied to offset trading fees.

Government-issued currency that is not backed by a physical commodity but derives value from trust and regulation (e.g., USD, EUR). Title case when referring to a MEXC product or zone; sentence case in general editorial use.
The final, actual price at which a trade is executed.
An order that must be executed in its entirety immediately; if not fully fillable, it is canceled completely.
The first official listing of a new token on the trading platform, marking its market debut.
A loan with a fixed loan duration where the interest rate is locked for the full term.
A MEXC Earn product with a fixed lockup duration, during which assets are frozen and cannot be withdrawn or traded.
An ad in which the advertiser sets a fixed price for cryptocurrency that remains unchanged regardless of market fluctuations.
A feature that closes a position quickly by matching against the best 30 available bid prices. Any unfilled portion is automatically converted into a limit order.
A Futures feature that lets traders open positions directly on the candlestick chart using market orders, without switching to the order panel.
The action of sharing an affiliate's Flex Strategy.
A feature that allows affiliates to select and automatically share their trading strategies with their group.
A loan with no fixed loan term. Users can borrow and repay at any time and the interest is calculated hourly.
A MEXC Earn product with no fixed lockup period; assets remain tradable and withdrawable anytime.
Users can redeem partial or full stakes at any time without lockup restrictions.
A trading event that uses a card-flipping mechanism; users earn flip opportunities by completing trading tasks.
Buying an asset with the intention of selling it quickly for a short-term profit, typically driven by rapid price movements.
An ad whose price is linked to the market rate.
The lowest price at which an asset, commonly an NFT, is currently available for sale in the market.
The act or order of copying a Trader's position or trade in the copy trading system.
A user who replicates or copies the trades of a designated Trader in a copy trading system.
The unique identification number or code assigned to a Follower in the system.
The global market for trading national fiat currencies (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).
Specialized derivative contracts on MEXC that allow users to speculate on the price movements of major national currencies (such as EUR, GBP, and JPY) using crypto as collateral.
Tokens can be frozen by authorized functions.
The total value of a cryptocurrency calculated by multiplying the current price by the maximum possible supply of tokens, assuming all tokens are in circulation.
Product is currently fully staked, no quota left.
Payments based on funding rate and position size, settled every 8 hours on MEXC, positive rate means longs pay shorts.
A periodic fee exchanged between long and short positions in leveraged token trading, influencing the cost of holding positions.
The MEXC Foundation's flagship global academic initiative, building long-term partnerships with universities and research institutions through funding, education, and innovation programs.
A type of finullcial derivative that allows users to trade the future price of an asset. Title case when referring to a MEXC product or zone; sentence case in general editorial use.
An Earn product for users who hold eligible assets in their Futures account to automatically earn daily interest.
The name of the Futures grid trading feature on MEXC and its entrance on the platform. The feature enables automated grid trading strategies on Futures markets.

AI leverages in-depth analysis of historical market data and real-time price trends for a given trading pair. It intelligently and automatically sets the optimal price range, number of grids, and leverage multiplier for the user.
A dedicated insurance fund providing protection to futures traders during periods of extreme market volatility or unexpected losses.

An ongoing MEXC Futures trading event that ranks participants by daily PNL and trading volume. Top-ranked users share daily prize pools, with rewards distributed across daily, weekly, and monthly periods.

Also referred to as M-Day. A special, futures-focused themed event day on the MEXC platform, featuring exclusive trading sessions, limited-time missions, leaderboard competitions, and bonus rewards centered on futures trading.
A trading bot within the Futures Grid feature that executes grid trading strategies on Futures, capturing profits by placing buy and sell orders within a predefined grid.
A multi-currency payment service facilitating P2P exchanges, cryptocurrency transactions, and conversions, often used for buying/selling assets like USDT at favorable rates.
The expected network (gas) fee cost for executing an on-chain transaction.
A rare reward found inside chests during M-Day events. Uncovering a Gem entitles the user to substantial Futures bonus rewards.
Generally displayed as a button. Directs users to customer support or assistance resources.
Navigates the user to a relevant page or next step.
Copy Trade Showdown's level-4 treasure chest reward.
A bullish technical signal that occurs when a short-term moving average crosses above a long-term moving average, suggesting potential upward price momentum.
An order that remains active until fully filled or manually canceled by the user, without an expiration time.
A bundle of tokens in varying amounts, e.g., 88 ABC; 8,888 CDE; 66,666 EFG.
Two-factor authentication using Google Authenticator or similar services for secure account access.
An app for generating 2FA codes, providing an extra layer of security beyond passwords.
A measure of the rate and direction of change, often used in data analysis or algorithmic trading to describe trends.
Generally displayed as a status label on token listing pages. Tokens or projects nearing full launch or listing status.
The number of discrete price intervals (grids) inside the price range where buy and sell orders are placed.
Unique identifier assigned to each grid order for tracking and management.
Settings for a grid bot strategy, including price range, number of grids, investment amount, leverage, stop prices, and activation price.
Net profit realized from grid trading.
The number of completed profitable trades (buy low, sell high) made by the grid bot.
Historical logs showing profitable exit trades executed by grid bot.
An automated trading bot that places buy and sell orders at preset price intervals within a defined range, profiting from market fluctuations without manual intervention.

Also displayed as "Guaranteed SL." A stop-loss order that guarantees position closure strictly at the trigger price, preventing additional losses from market slippage. An extra trading fee is charged upon successful execution.

A ranking system in Flip Fest that assigns point values to card hands based on their combination, ranging from C+ (lowest) to S (highest). Higher-ranked hands earn more points and a greater share of the prize pool.
Subtle vibrations or tactile responses when you interact with the app, confirming actions through touch.
A significant blockchain protocol change that creates a permanent split, resulting in two separate chains if not universally adopted.
A measure of computational power used to process transactions and secure a blockchain network, typically expressed as hashes per second.
A position mode permitting simultaneous long and short positions in the same trading pair.
Option to filter out or hide trading pairs not currently of interest.
A label to describe the Trader's style: Trading strategy involves executing many trades within short timeframes, often aiming to profit from small price movements.
Generally displayed as a risk status label on token or contract pages. Token or contract assessed to have high risk levels.
An earn product that allows users to generate interest on their crypto holdings.
Generally displayed as a data field on token detail pages. Number of wallets or addresses holding a particular token.
A token that has restrictions preventing holders from selling, indicating potential scam.
A trading event focused on specific trending or volatile cryptocurrency pairs in the futures market.
Frequently searched tokens or pairs.
The hourly cost of borrowing for Flexible Loans, applied to the outstanding loan amount. Any period less than one full hour is counted as a full hour.
An order that executes immediately for whatever quantity is available; any unfilled portion is cancelled at once.
A temporary loss experienced by liquidity providers due to price fluctuations between assets in a liquidity pool.
Generally displayed as an order status label in P2P. Indicates that the buyer and seller are communicating to resolve an issue or dispute before further action is taken.
Status indicating a prediction was incorrect.
A weighted average price from major exchanges, serving as a benchmark for fair price calculations in futures.
The LTV at the time the loan is opened, calculated based on the borrowed amount and the collateral value when the loan starts.
The amount of capital, denominated in USDT or any other units, initially allocated to the bot when creating the grid trading strategy.
Replaced with: First in Market. The first official listing of a new token on the trading platform, marking its market debut.
The minimum collateral required to open a position, determined by leverage and position value.
Percentage showing margin usage, calculated as Initial margin / Effective margin.
A Convert order type where the order is filled immediately.
Sub-accounts designed for institutional investors, offering advanced features like higher limits and segregated fund management.
Notification that user asset balance is not enough to complete a trade or fee payment.
A reserve fund used by the exchange to cover losses from liquidations that fail to close at bankruptcy price, preventing ADL.
The cost of borrowing funds or liabilities, or the earnings generated from staked assets, calculated over time.
Total distributed interest from the MEXC Earn products.
Current est. interest accrual time.
Total rewards earned from staking.
Amount of interest credited/distributed.
The process of calculating and settling interest on borrowed funds or liabilities.
Records of past interest settlements on liabilities or borrowed funds.
Detailed log of interest distributions.
The portion of your staked asset currently generating interest.
The principal used for interest calculation is the average value based on multiple snapshots taken during the interest accrual period, ensuring a fair and consistent calculation.
The price of an asset at any point during the current trading day.
The investment allocation for each asset. Total percentage must be 100%.
Initiates an invitation action, such as inviting users to join MEXC.
Former term for a user who joins MEXC via a referral link or code. Replaced by Referee
Former term for a user who refers others to MEXC via a referral link or code. Replaced by Referrer.
Copy Trade Showdown's level-1 treasure chest reward.
Mode allocating fixed margin to a specific position, limiting risk to that margin on liquidation.
Margin mode where each position has its own independent margin.
Initiates the action of enrolling in a campaign or program.
The process of verifying a user's identity to comply with regulatory requirements.
A MEXC pre-listing campaign where users commit MX tokens to vote for new projects and receive free token airdrops upon successful listing.
Loan-to-Value ratio on MEXC, calculated as outstanding principal and interest divided by collateral value, determining liquidation risk.
The most recent executed trade price of an asset.
A MEXC token subscription platform that gives users early access to promising new projects and established tokens at favorable prices.
A MEXC airdrop platform where users stake supported tokens such as MX or USDT to earn free airdrops of trending or newly listed cryptocurrencies.
The original trade placed by a Trader that is copied by Followers.
A higher tier in the MEXC P2P Merchant Program for merchants who have exceeded Prime Merchant thresholds in completion rate, trading volume, and completed orders.
A Futures setting that determines how leverage and margin are applied across positions. Choose between Simple Mode and Advanced Mode.
Outstanding debts or obligations in the account.
Collection of funds to cover liabilities owed in the account.
Payment made to repay outstanding liabilities in the account.
A Convert order type where the order is filled when the fair price reaches the set limit price.
An order to buy or sell at a specified price or better, which may not execute immediately if the market doesn't reach that price.
The specified price at which a limit order is set to execute, ensuring the trade occurs at that price or better.
Forced closure of a position when margin falls below maintenullce level, to prevent further losses.
The LTV level at which user's collateral may be automatically sold to repay the loan if no action is taken after a margin call.
Mostly used in DEX. A security status signifying liquidity funds are locked and cannot be withdrawn immediately.
Pool of tokens providing liquidity for trading pairs on the DEX+.
A user who supplies assets to a liquidity pool to facilitate trading on decentralized platforms and earn fees or rewards.
Generally displayed as a status label on token listing pages. Tokens officially listed and available for trading on the platform.
MEXC App Lite, or Lite App is a streamlined version of the MEXC App designed for faster performance, simpler navigation, and essential trading features.
A pass that grants access to real-time stock market data, helping users make informed trading decisions.
Real-time trading with actual funds on the exchange, as opposed to demo mode, involving genuine market risks and rewards.
The ratio of your borrowed amount to the value of your collateral, expressed as a percentage.
A mechanism where users must lock their crypto assets for a specified period to participate in events, earn higher yields, or receive rewards, with assets unavailable for withdrawal during lockup.
A trading stance on the price of an asset rising. The grid is optimized to benefit from upward price trends. Capitalize if it appears as a button.
Expected price fluctuations or market volatility over a long-term horizon, generally spanning 3–6 months or longer.
An indicator showing the proportion of open positions betting on the price going up (Long) versus going down (Short).
A protection mechanism that compensates users with a Futures bonus if a trade results in a loss, up to a specified coverage limit.
A label to describe the Trader's style: Trading strategy that executes a small number of trades over a longer period, focusing on fewer but often larger opportunities.
Generally displayed as a risk status label on token or contract pages. Token or contract assessed to have low risk levels.
A bot setting that reduces investment requirements and manages risk exposure, possibly at the expense of reduced investment scalability.
Min. price level set by the user that defines the lower boundary of the grid trading range.
A user-defined price level below which the bot will stop trading to limit losses or lock in profits.

Also referred to as Futures M-Day. A special, futures-focused themed event day on the MEXC platform, featuring exclusive trading sessions, limited-time missions, leaderboard competitions, and bonus rewards centered on futures trading.
Protection mechanism mitigating Miner Extractable Value (MEV) attacks during trades.
The overarching master AI feature of the MEXC exchange, integrating AI capabilities to enhance trading, market analysis, and user experience.

MEXC Affiliate Program is designed for those who want to monetize their influence by promoting MEXC. Affiliates earn competitive commissions every time someone they refer signs up and trades, with earnings that scale as their network grows.
A suite of savings products that allows users to earn interest on their crypto assets.
The Event Center is a dedicated page on MEXC where users can browse current, upcoming, and past events by tradução, as well as view the events they have registered for.
MEXC Global Ambassador Program is a global network of traders, creators, and community builders who represent MEXC across three pillars—trading credibility, brand narrative, and community trust.
A debit card issued by MEXC designed to make crypto more accessible for everyday spending, targeting users who are less familiar with crypto.
MEXC Learn is the platform's educational hub, offering articles, guides, and resources to help users understand cryptocurrency, trading, and blockchain concepts.
categoria: MEXC 学院 UGC 创作者计划

descrição: MEXC Learn UGC Creator Program invites writers and researchers to contribute original crypto and Web3 content to MEXC Learn, MEXC's global knowledge platform, earning rewards based on content quality and readership performance.
A MEXC Earn product that allows users to borrow USDT or USDC using crypto as collateral.
Allows users to become verified P2P merchants, facilitating crypto trades and earning commissions.

The MEXC Referral Ambassador Program allows any user to earn commissions by referring friends to the platform. Ambassadors are organized into three tiers, Rising, Elite, and Champion, based on referral activity, with each tier corresponding to different commission rates and benefits.

The MEXC Referral Program rewards users for growing the MEXC community. Users share their unique referral link or code, earn when the people they bring in deposit and trade, and keep earning as those users stay active on the platform.

A tiered membership program for high-volume traders. VIP levels are automatically assigned based on total trading volume over the past 30 days and come with exclusive benefits including reduced trading fees and dedicated account managers.
The exchange's proprietary AI chatbot designed to assist users by answering questions, providing trading guidance, and offering personalized support within MEXC.
A term for members of the MEXC user community.
Current MEXC brand slogan.
A former MEXC reward currency redeemable for discounts or benefits, similar to a coupon.
A dedicated MEXC page where users can explore the full range of MX token holder benefits.
MEXC's liquid staking token representing staked SOL on the platform; redeemable for SOL plus rewards.
A user's primary account, used to manage assets, sub-accounts, and platform features.
The process of combining or upgrading blockchain networks, often involving transitioning consensus mechanisms or infrastructure.
The minimum equity needed to keep a position open; falling below triggers liquidation.
Percentage indicating account risk level, calculated as (Position maintenullce margin + Liability maintenullce margin) / Effective margin.
Customer support releases crypto after dispute.
The action of setting up a copy trade or trading bot by entering parameters manually instead of using AI recommendations.
A predefined LTV threshold that triggers a warning, prompting users to add more collateral or repay part of the loan to avoid liquidation.
The ratio of maintenullce margin to total account margin in a leveraged position, indicating how close a position is to liquidation. The higher the ratio, the greater the liquidation risk.
Displayed as Fair price on MEXC. The reference price used for calculating unrealized PNL and determining liquidations, derived from index and market prices to prevent price manipulation.
The total market value of a token, calculated as price multiplied by circulating supply.
The percentage or absolute change in market cap over the specified time.
A user or entity that provides liquidity by placing continuous buy and sell orders on the platform.
An account type used by market makers.
An order to buy or sell immediately at the current market price.
Markets has long been the name of a main page on both the App and the Web. Recently, there was a change in the Chinese version, but it should not affect the existing name for Markets.
A primary account that controls or manages multiple sub-accounts, often used for consolidated trading or fund management.
Event name. And also the honored title for top participants excelling in accurate price movement predictions on BTC and ETH.
Profit or loss from trade pairs (buy followed by sell) successfully completed by the bot.

Pre-Market orders paired with opposing orders and confirmed for execution when trading conditions are met, or opposing positions (e.g., long and short) that are internally paired to reduce exposure and system risk during liquidation or risk management processes.
The date when the savings product or staking period reaches its full term and the principal becomes available for withdrawal.
The standard abbreviation for the maximum allowable value or amount.

termo: Maximum sell ratio restriction (Max. sell ratio restriction)
descrição: Maximum drawdown is the largest peak-to-trough decline in an asset’s or portfolio’s value over a given period, measured as a percentage.
Limits on the maximum portion of tokens a holder can sell at once.
The maximum number of tokens that will ever be issued or mined.
A dismissal option that defers a decision or action to a later time.
Generally displayed as a risk status label on token or contract pages. Token or contract assessed to have moderate risk levels.
A dedicated MEXC trading zone for early-stage and emerging memecoins.
A dedicated dashboard where merchants manage their listings, account settings, and trading activity.

A Merkle tree is a cryptographic data structure that securely summarizes large amounts of data by hashing them into a single root value, enabling efficient verification of data integrity without revealing the underlying details.
MetaMask is a digital wallet that allows users to store cryptocurrencies, interact with blockchain applications, and manage assets on Ethereum and other compatible networks.
Precious metal assets such as gold and silver, available for trading on MEXC via both Spot and Futures markets. When used as a market zone header, written in title case as "Metals".
A virtual digital environment where users can interact, socialize, and transact using avatars and digital assets.
Expected price fluctuations or market volatility over a mid-term period, often ranging from 1–2 months.
Token contract feature enabling new token supply to be minted.
Registration process using a mobile phone number for account creation.
Whether contract owner can modify token balances arbitrarily.
A technical analysis tool that measures the speed and strength of price movements to help identify trends or potential reversals.
Directs users to additional information or content.
A blockchain-based model where users earn rewards by performing physical activities such as walking or exercising.
The ability to manage and operate multiple accounts simultaneously, often for diversification or institutional fund management.
Assets pledged as collateral in multi-asset mode.
The current account assets available as margin in multi-asset mode for opening positions, valued in USD.
A margin mode allowing multiple asset types to be used as collateral and share margin across positions.
A crypto wallet that requires multiple private keys to authorize a transaction, increasing security.
Amount of crypto currently staked.
A situation where losses exceed the initial margin or account balance, resulting in a negative equity position that the trader owes to the platform.
A funding rate condition where short position holders pay funding fees to long position holders.
The final difference between total assets transferred in and total assets transferred out of an account.
A trading stance where the bot operates without a directional bias, often placing both buy and sell orders symmetrically within the grid.
Staked tokens are not locked, allowing anytime redemption or transfer.
The total value of a position or contract based on its size and price, also known as notional value.
Indicates that tokens cannot be frozen by any contract function.
A unique digital asset stored on a blockchain that represents ownership of a specific item or content and cannot be exchanged one-to-one like cryptocurrencies.
Payment methods that require processing time.

A participation mechanism that does not require users to lock their assets, allowing normal use or transfer while still qualifying for event rewards. Commonly seen in MEXC Launchpool and Kickstarter events.
Tokens that cannot be freshly minted by contract owners.
A MEXC Earn product that allows users to stake crypto and participate in DeFi protocols directly on-chain, offering high-yield opportunities.
A pair of linked orders where executing one order automatically cancels the other.
A position mode allowing only one direction (long or short) per trading pair at a time.
Pending limit orders placed by the grid bot within the defined price grids.
An action to buy and open a long position, anticipating that the asset price will rise. Sometimes shown as "Open Long (Buy).
An order that has been placed but not yet filled or cancelled.
An active futures holding that has been entered but not yet closed, viewable in the positions tab.
An action to sell and open a short position, anticipating that the asset price will fall. Sometimes shown as "Open Short (Sell).
Features related to placing and managing trade orders.
Generally displayed as a section label in the trading interface. Record of past placed orders, including completed and cancelled.
The remaining unpaid principal and accrued interest on a MEXC loan, tracked in the user's loan dashboard.
A market condition where an asset's price has risen too quickly and may be due for a correction or pullback.
A market condition where an asset's price has fallen too sharply and may be due for a rebound.
Proportion of total tokens held by the contract owner.
Short for peer-to-peer; a trading model where users buy and sell crypto directly with each other without using a centralized order book.
Used instead of PnL, P&L.

A Futures trading method where unrealized profits from open positions are used as margin to open or increase additional positions, without adding new capital. Also referred to as a rolling position strategy.
Profit and loss expressed as a percentage of initial capital or position size.
Uses the device's camera to verify user identity by scanning the unique features of the user's palm for secure authentication.
Refers to a portion of an open position, used when closing or adjusting only part of a trade.
A passwordless authentication method that allows users to log in using biometrics (fingerprint, facial recognition) or device PINs.
The act of pausing the current ongoing Spot DCA plan.
The overall rate of return provided when a prediction is successful, regardless of direction.
The actual amount paid out to the trader upon a successful prediction.
Aggregate data summarizing the bot's trading performance, including profit/loss, number of executed trades, fees paid, and other related statistics.
The maximum staking allowance a user currently has available based on quota left.
A type of cyberattack where attackers impersonate trusted entities to trick users into revealing sensitive information such as passwords or private keys.
Submission of a government-issued photo ID for identity verification during KYC or account setup processes.
A gaming model where players earn cryptocurrency or digital assets through gameplay participation and achievements.
Earned by completing Flip Fest trading tasks; can be used to win event rewards.
Points are a reward currency earned through tasks on MEXC DEX+, redeemable for airdrops or token rewards. Some campaigns also use points for event-specific prizes.
A multiplier that increases the points earned, often through daily challenges, referrals, or hand rank milestones.
Generally displayed as a section label or data field in the trading interface. Active token holding or open trade position.
Generally displayed as a data field in the trading interface. Profit or loss realized or unrealized on current positions.
A type of reward given in the form of open positions in a Futures trading pair. Sometimes referred to as Position airdrop voucher.
The allocated funds required to hold an open position, including initial margin and any adjustments, varying by isolated or cross mode.
A Futures setting that determines how positions are managed — either hedge mode (long and short simultaneously) or one-way mode (one direction only).
Generally displayed as a data field in Futures. Number of contracts.
Generally displayed as a data field in the trading interface. The current total market value of held positions.
A funding rate condition where long position holders pay funding fees to short position holders.
A limit order condition that ensures it adds liquidity as a maker; if it would immediately match and take, it cancels instead.
Special cards earned by reaching Futures trading volume milestones during Flip Fest, used to swap and upgrade a user's card hand for higher points and a larger share of the prize pool.
Trading conducted before an asset is officially listed for regular trading on the platform.
The temporary addition of funds to your account before the deposit process is fully completed or confirmed.
categoria: 预测 BTC 及 ETH 走勢

descrição: Event title. To forecast whether the prices of Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) will rise or fall within a chosen timeframe.
Outcome of the Event Futures contract (whether the trader's forecast was correct or incorrect).
Another name for the entrance to the MEXC-AI chat session, displayed on the trading page in the carousel area.
A feature that rejects TP/SL or trigger orders when the difference between the last price and the fair price exceeds a preset threshold, protecting users from executing at abnormal prices.
A price quote is the current price at which an asset can be bought or sold in the market.
The lower and upper price boundaries within which the bot places limit orders as part of the grid strategy.
The first level of identity verification that grants users access to enhanced trading permissions and Savings events.
A mid-level tier in the MEXC P2P Merchant Program for merchants who have met specific performance criteria, including minimum completion rate, trading volume, and order count.
The original amount of capital deposited in the MEXC Earn product.
The percentage of profits allocated between the Trader and Followers in copy trading.
A consensus mechanism where validators stake cryptocurrency to participate in transaction validation and earn rewards.
A certification or guarantee indicating the platform's commitment to asset security, backed by transparent reserve holdings.
A blockchain consensus mechanism where miners solve complex computational problems to validate transactions and secure the network.
The underlying blockchain or staking protocol/mechanism used.
Smart contract utilizing a proxy pattern allowing upgradability.
A blockchain network that is open to anyone to participate in, without centralized control.
The open phase of a token sale where the general public can purchase tokens.
Generally displayed as a data field in order details. Price paid per token during purchase.
A contract or position that gives the holder the right (but not the obligation) to sell an asset at a specified price within a certain time period.
Generally displayed as a data field in order and trade details. Number of tokens involved in a trade or order.
A fast-track option to pledge or commit funds to a project or launchpad event, often with time-limited slots for participation.
Feature allowing swift token purchase with 1-click.
A feature enabling fast and easy copying of a Trader’s trades with 1 click.
One-click staking with minimal user steps or complexity.
The currency used to express the price of the base asset in a trading pair.
Return on Investment. A performance metric calculated as (Total Realized PNL ÷ Total Copy Trade Cost) × 100%, representing profit relative to investment.
An entry pass used in the MEXC Race to Zero event that grants one attempt at the countdown game. Earned by completing daily, leaderboard, or exclusive tasks.
A shortened event name for Race to Zero: Win 1 BTC in 8 Seconds.
Physical or traditional finullcial assets represented on the blockchain, enabling them to be traded, finullced, or used within digital markets.
Real-time principal is the current value. Interest is calculated based on the average of 3 snapshots from the previous day.
The actual profit or loss booked after closing a position or part of it, including fees and funding.
A partial refund of trading fees, distributed as a reward. On MEXC, rebates appear as broker rewards based on client trading activity or as campaign incentives.
Section highlighting select Earn products recommended by the platform.
The action of withdrawing funds from a MEXC Earn product.
An option or action to redeem Fixed Savings products before maturity, often with penalties.
Status indicating assets have been successfully redeemed.
Status indicating redemption operation is in progress.
Notification that a redemption request was unsuccessful.
Timestamp of when a redemption occurred.
The portion of margin that can be safely withdrawn or reduced without affecting the stability of an open position.
Initiates the action of referring a new user via a referral link or code.
A user who signs up for MEXC using another user's referral code or link.
A unique alphanumeric code assigned to each MEXC user that can be shared to invite others to sign up. New users enter this code during registration to link their account to the referrer.
A unique URL assigned to each MEXC user that automatically applies the referrer's code when clicked. New users who sign up via the link are automatically linked to the referrer.
Bonuses or commissions earned by inviting new users to join and trade on the platform.
Task rewarding users for inviting friends to the platform or event.
A user who invites others to join MEXC by sharing their referral code or link, earning commissions from the referee's trading activity.
Generally displayed as a button. The action of enrolling in a specific MEXC event or campaign. Not to be confused with Sign Up, which refers to creating a new account.
The payment of the borrowed amount along with any accrued interest, either partially or in full.
Process to change or recover security settings, requiring verification to prevent unauthorized modifications.
A price level where selling pressure tends to emerge, preventing the asset from rising further.
The conversion rate showing the equivalence in the reverse direction (to-coin to from-coin).
A trading or conversion mechanism where assets are exchanged in the opposite direction of a standard pair or process.
A Futures action that closes the current position and simultaneously opens an opposite position of the same size at market price.
Futures settings under Preferences. When enabled, if funds are insufficient to open the same quantity of reverse positions, the reverse order will not be filled.
Corporate actions that reduce the number of outstanding shares by consolidating multiple shares into one, affecting futures contracts settlement.
The page where users can view their rewards, complete tasks, and discover events. (Formerly Rewards Center; term deprecated.)
MXSOL value increases over time relative to SOL as staking rewards accrue; conversion rate updates every Solana epoch (~2-3 days).
A mid-tier prize pool in Flip Fest for participants who meet higher criteria than the Starter Pool.
Tiered limits on position size affecting maximum leverage; larger positions lower available leverage.
A single and complete investment process within the broader DCA plan.
Indicates the duration since the grid bot has started running (has been active).
Refers to how the grid bot is running, such as Close Positions on Termination or Arithmetic.
Displays the number of active grid bots.
Identity confirmation via codes sent through text messages, commonly used for 2FA.
A verification method sending one-time codes via SMS for login or transaction confirmation, enhancing account security.
A MEXC liquid staking product that allows users to stake SOL tokens in exchange for MXSOL, a liquid staking token that accumulates staking rewards over time and can be redeemed for SOL.
A risk warning tag indicating that a trading pair does not meet the platform’s liquidity or listing requirements and may be subject to increased monitoring or potential delisting.
A sub-account integrated with SafePal hardware wallet for enhanced security, enabling cold storage management within an exchange environment.
Funds temporarily frozen by the platform as a safeguard against rule violations. It may be required from merchants or users and can be forfeited for misconduct.
A sequence of words generated for wallet recovery, serving as a backup to regain access to cryptocurrency holdings if keys are lost.
Contract feature allowing it to be destroyed and removed from blockchain.
Generally displayed as a data field in order details. Price at which tokens were sold.
The final amount settled at the close of an Event Futures contract, reflecting realized profit or loss after settlement.
Generally displayed as a data field in Multi-asset mode. The currency used to settle trades and calculate margin requirements.
Generally displayed as a data field in Multi-asset mode. The interest rate applied during settlement of liabilities.
Generally displayed as a data field in Multi-asset mode. The time when interest or liabilities are settled.
The Sharpe ratio measures risk-adjusted return by comparing an investment's excess return to its volatility. A higher value indicates better return for the level of risk taken.
A trading stance on the price of an asset falling. Grid bots operating short profit from declining asset prices. Capitalize if it appears as a button.
Expected price fluctuations or market volatility over a short to mid-term period, typically within 7–30 days.
Generally displayed as a button. The action of creating a new MEXC account. Not to be confused with Register, which refers to joining a specific event or campaign.
Copy Trade Showdown's level-3 treasure chest reward.
A leverage mode where the same leverage and margin settings apply to both long and short positions.
The current account assets available as margin in single-asset mode for opening positions, valued in USD.
A margin mode allowing only a single asset type to be used as collateral.
The difference between expected and actual trade execution price.
A feature under AI Analysis. It analyzes market-moving news and data in real time, estimating the probability of upcoming price movements to help you anticipate market direction.
Under Smart Candles. An AI-enhanced chart view that overlays market-moving events and signals to help interpret price action more effectively.
A self-executing program on a blockchain that automatically enforces the terms of an agreement when predefined conditions are met.

Smart copy allows users to automatically copy the trading decisions of AI models without manual execution. It is a general act of AI-powered copy trading, and the proper product name is AI Model Copy Trade.
Wallets or entities known for informed or large-volume trading activities on-chain.
An option to place orders using the same funding ratio as the Trader you follow, ensuring proportional trade scaling.
A recorded state of a blockchain at a specific block height or time.
Status indicating no remaining quota in the staking product; no new staking can be accepted.
Arrangement of items based on a chosen criterion, e.g., price or volume.
A primary trading product where assets are bought or sold for immediate settlement. Usually displayed in Title Case.
A systematic investment strategy that automatically purchases your chosen cryptocurrencies at predetermined intervals and amounts within your specified price range.
The name of the Spot grid trading feature on MEXC and its entrance on the platform. The feature enables automated grid trading strategies on the Spot market.
A type of reward distributed as free tokens directly to users' Spot wallets. It is withdrawable, unlike a bonus.
A trading bot within the Spot Grid feature that executes grid trading strategies on Spot assets, capturing profits by placing buy and sell orders within a predefined grid.
The difference between the buying price (bid) and selling price (ask) of an asset in the market.
A type of cryptocurrency designed to maintain a stable value, usually pegged to fiat currency or other assets.
Initiates the action of staking assets in an Earn product.
The amount of crypto staked for a MEXC Earn product.
User confirmation prompt before submitting staking transaction.
categoria: 平台质押量已达产品质押上限

descrição: Product staking limit reached; no further staking accepted.
Temporary halt on staking activity due to updates or maintenullce.
A group of participants who combine their staking resources to increase the chances of earning rewards in Proof of Stake networks.
Notification confirming successful staking operation.
Timestamp of when a staking was made.
Standard mode: responses will be simpler and easier to understand. A feature under MEXC-AI.
The entry-level tier in the MEXC P2P Merchant Program for users who have not applied for merchant verification.
A basic sub-account type for general use, allowing separation of funds and activities from the main account.
The entry-level prize pool tier in Flip Fest, open to all participants.

Stock Futures are a type of finullcial derivative offered by MEXC that link U.S. stocks (shares of companies listed in the United States) with the cryptocurrency market. They allow investors to use cryptocurrencies—such as USDT—as margin to trade on the price movements of U.S. stocks without actually owning the underlying shares.
Corporate actions that alter the number of outstanding shares, affecting futures contracts settlement.
Shares of US-listed companies available for trading on MEXC, primarily as perpetual futures pairs. When used as a market zone header, written in title case as "Stocks".
A stop order that, when triggered, places a limit order at a specified price for controlled execution.
A conditional order to close a position at a specified price to limit losses if the market moves adversely.
Indicates that a bot or trading strategy has been halted or is no longer active.
A specialized sub-account designed for executing specific trading strategies, allowing isolation of risks and performance tracking separate from the main account.
Secondary accounts under a main account.
A referral relationship between two affiliates, where the sub-affiliate was recruited by another affiliate and generates additional commission for the original affiliate.
A recurring weekly MEXC Futures trading event where users compete for a share of a prize pool by trading with 21x leverage or higher.
A price level where buying interest is strong enough to prevent the asset from falling further.
Copy Trade Showdown's level-6 treasure chest reward.
The last digit of a ticket number or transaction ID used in MEXC lottery-style events to determine winners through matching with a drawn digit.
A conditional order to close a position at a specified price to lock in profits when the market moves in the trader's favor.
A mechanism where a portion of transaction fees or tokens is permanently removed from circulation to reduce supply.
The duration for which assets are staked in Fixed Savings products.
A button or action that ends or cancels a service, account status, or active process.
A predefined value or level that, when reached or crossed, triggers a signal, action, or change in market interpretation.
A virtual credential earned by completing tasks, trading, or referrals, used to join lucky draws, claim rewards, or share prize pools. Commonly used in M-Day and Launchpad events.
Different levels or categories of tickets in MEXC events, corresponding to varying reward values or probabilities, such as basic vs. premium.
A specific rule (often based on trading volume or registration time) used to determine the winner when two participants have the same ranking score.
The date and time when a trade or transaction occurred.
Pending funds or assets earmarked for commitment to a project or event, awaiting confirmation or processing before being locked in.
Generally displayed as a data field in the loan dashboard. The total amount, including principal and interest, that a user must repay to close a loan on MEXC and release collateral.
The process on MEXC of updating a token to a new network or contract, often involving deposit suspension and automatic swaps for users.
An exchange of old tokens for new ones during migrations on MEXC, typically at a 1:1 ratio, to support project upgrades.
A voucher redeemable for a designated cryptocurrency, valid for 14 days from issue.
The economic model of a cryptocurrency, including supply, distribution, incentives, and utility within its ecosystem.
The total value of user assets calculated in USDT, including funds allocated to various bots, trading positions, and available balances.
The cumulative amount spent buying tokens.
Total funding (interest) fees accrued during operation, associated with leveraged futures positions.
The total amount of capital invested or allocated over the lifetime of the grid bot’s operation.
The overall market capitalization of all tokens on the platform or a pair.
Total cumulative amount gained from selling tokens.
The total current value of assets held by the grid bot, including unrealized profits and losses.
The total value of assets deposited in a decentralized finullce (DeFi) protocol, typically used to measure its size and activity.
The total amount of funds withdrawn from the grid bot during its operation.
Biometric authentication using fingerprint scanning for secure login and transaction approvals on compatible devices.
Navigates the user to the trading interface for a selected asset.
Generally displayed as a data field in the trading interface. Total monetary value of trades executed.
Generally displayed as a section label in the trading interface. Log of all past trades executed on the platform.
Generally displayed as a data field in order details. Classification of the trade such as buy or sell.
Generally displayed as a data field in the trading interface. Quantity of tokens traded within a given period.
An experienced Trader whose trades can be automatically copied by Followers in real time.
A generic term for spot/futures grid trading used in articles or CS responses.
How often a Trader executes trades within a given time period, indicating activity level.
The price distance or percentage retracement from the market high/low that triggers a trailing stop order to execute.
The price offset or distance used in trailing stop orders to determine how far the stop price follows the market price.
An order that adjusts the stop price as the market moves favorably, locking in profits while allowing for further gains; trigger calculated from high/low ± variance.
The movement of assets between a user's own MEXC accounts, such as from Spot to Futures or Fiat to Spot.
Ability for contract owner to pause token transfers.
An order that is automatically placed when the market reaches a specified trigger price, using a pre-set order price and quantity. Margin and positions are not frozen until the order is activated.
The predefined market price that activates a conditional order, such as TP/SL or trailing stop, to execute.
A conditional order that places a limit order when a trigger price is reached, combining trigger and limit functionalities.
A conditional order that places a market order when a trigger price is hit, for immediate execution at prevailing prices.
Unique transaction hash on the blockchain for a trade or transfer.
Shares of publicly traded companies listed on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq.
USDC-Margined. Linear perpetual futures margined and settled in USDC, allowing stablecoin-based trading.
USDT-Margined. Linear perpetual futures margined and settled in USDT, allowing stablecoin-based trading.
The top-tier prize pool in Flip Fest offering the biggest rewards for participants who meet the highest criteria.
An Uncredited Deposit Return Application is a request submitted to recover and return funds from a deposit that was not credited to your account.
An uncredited deposit is a deposit that has been sent to the platform but has not yet been reflected in your account balance.
Generally displayed as an order status label in P2P. Indicates that the order has been formally appealed and is currently under review by the platform.
Profit or loss from active positions or orders not yet closed or paired.
The current paper profit or loss on an open position, calculated using mark price or last price. Used instead of floating PNL.
A prediction that the underlying asset’s price will increase during the contract period.
The rate of return a trader receives if the asset price goes up and their prediction in Event Futures is correct.
Transfer fees set by owner can be increased or modified.
Max. price level set by the user that defines the upper boundary of the grid trading range.
A user-defined price level above which the bot will stop trading to limit losses or lock in profits.
Virtual Payment Address, a unique identifier used in digital payment systems like UPI for seamless fund transfers without sharing bank details.
The date when funds are considered officially available or applied to the user account.
A one-time code sent via email, SMS, or app for confirming identity during logins, transactions, or changes.
The first verified tier in the MEXC P2P Merchant Program, with a higher ad posting limit than Standard Account.
Real-time or recorded video submission for identity verification, often used in advanced KYC processes.
Expands or navigates to a full list or additional content beyond what is currently shown.
An alias for bank accounts in payment networks, enabling easy and secure transactions via apps like those supporting India's UPI system.
Voice input allows you to ask questions using your voice instead of typing. MEXC AI converts your speech into text and provides instant, AI-generated answers.
A dedicated page where users can view, manage, and redeem all their vouchers, including expiry dates and claim status.
A manually reviewed section within MEXC P2P where ads are vetted for payment compliance and trade structure.
A list of approved addresses, users, or entities that are granted specific permissions or access.
The percentage of trades or positions that result in a profit compared to the total number of trades executed.
The blockchain network selected for transferring funds out of an account.
Multiple or sequential withdrawal processes, often referring to batch processing or repeated attempts in crypto transactions.
Total interest distributed to user's Spot account from 16:00 (UTC) yesterday to 15:59 (UTC) today.
A DeFi strategy where users provide liquidity to protocols in exchange for rewards, often in the form of tokens or interest.
The annualized return or interest rate earned on staked, loaned, or saved assets on MEXC, often expressed as APY in savings or lending products.

termo: Your Zero-Fee Gateway to Infinite Opportunities
descrição: Former MEXC brand slogan.
categoria: 通往无限机遇的零手续费门户
